amendments.body: src/adapters/fs.rs:3034-3055 still records the current snapshot hash on brand-new `TaskRunEntry` rows whenever the caller omits `plan_hash`. The comment at lines 3037-3042 already describes the bug: if `persist_plan` runs after dispatch but before `record_task_run_start`, the start row is stamped with the newer snapshot hash even though the bead was launched under the older plan. That leaves permanently incorrect lineage and journal data for the attempt, so the new-row auto-population path is not actually safe.
amendments.rationale: This breaks plan-to-execution lineage whenever planning advances between dispatch and start persistence.

amendments.body: src/contexts/milestone_record/model.rs:286-290 documents `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation == None` as "legacy entries that predate this field", but src/adapters/fs.rs:2608-2615 treats `None` as proof that the row was created before any plan existed and immediately backfills the current snapshot hash. For real legacy data, those are not equivalent: an older open row created under plan V1 will also have `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation=None`, so replaying or completing it after the snapshot advances to V2 will silently relabel the attempt with V2. The new provenance guard therefore does not protect existing lineages from plan-evolution corruption.
amendments.rationale: Historical open rows can be rewritten to the wrong plan on first repair, which defeats the safety goal of the added provenance field.

payload.summary: Found two correctness gaps in the new plan-hash lineage logic: new rows can still be stamped with the wrong plan after a plan update, and the new provenance field misclassifies legacy rows so historical attempts can be rebound to the current snapshot.
